q1.text: How closely do you follow sustainability and social issues?
q1.opt0: I don't really deal with them, I have my own problems.
q1.opt1: I sometimes read an article, but I don't dive deep into them.
q1.opt2: I inform myself regularly, and the current situation worries me.
q1.opt3: I actively look for solutions and positive examples.

q2.text: To what extent are you willing to change your consumption habits for the sake of the environment or society?
q2.opt0: Not at all, comfort is the most important for me.
q2.opt1: If it doesn't involve great sacrifice or extra cost, then yes.
q2.opt2: I consciously watch what I buy and am willing to pay more for sustainable products.
q2.opt3: I have fundamentally reorganized my lifestyle in the spirit of sustainability (e.g., zero waste, veganism, energy independence).

q3.text: How often do you share information or publicly stand up for an important social or ecological cause?
q3.opt1: I only talk about such things in a narrow circle of friends.
q3.opt2: I often share articles, petitions on social media, and I take a stand.
q3.opt3: I also organize campaigns, write articles, or give lectures myself.

q4.text: How much do you participate in the life of your local community or in volunteer work?
q4.opt0: Not at all, I don't have the time or energy.
q4.opt1: Rarely, I participate in a cleanup or charity action if invited.
q4.opt2: I regularly volunteer at an NGO or in a local initiative.
q4.opt3: I start and lead local community projects myself (e.g., community garden, fundraising).

q5.text: Have you ever developed your own new solution to a problem?
q5.opt0: No, I usually follow proven paths.
q5.opt1: I usually introduce minor innovations in my work or private life.
q5.opt2: Yes, I developed a usable novelty for a small community or organization.
q5.opt3: I have created an innovative model/project that has a measurable, wider impact.

q6.text: When faced with a problem, what time horizons do you usually think about for the solution?
q6.opt0: How I can get rid of the current inconvenience as quickly as possible.
q6.opt1: How I can solve it so that there are no problems with it for a while.
q6.opt2: I look for a solution that works permanently, for years, understanding the causes of the problem.
q6.opt3: I think at a system level and try to change the root of the problem and the underlying structures, focusing on the coming decades.

q7.text: How much are you able to mobilize and coordinate resources (people, money, knowledge) for a goal?
q7.opt0: I rather work alone, I'm not good at organizing.
q7.opt1: I can involve a few acquaintances in some small things.
q7.opt2: I am able to lead and motivate a team, or obtain smaller grants.
q7.opt3: I successfully mobilize larger masses, coordinate multiple organizations, and / or involve significant resources.

q8.text: At what level does your networking ability operate?
q8.opt0: I don't like getting to know people, I only communicate with those I already know well.
q8.opt1: I am open to new connections if I meet like-minded people.
q8.opt2: I actively build my network with representatives of different fields to achieve my own goal.
q8.opt3: My main activity is connecting people, projects, and systems, building alliances and platforms for common synergies.

q9.text: What do you see as your personal main task, vocation?
q9.opt0: Surviving everyday life and taking care of my family.
q9.opt1: Doing work that doesn't harm, and enjoying life in the meantime.
q9.opt2: Actively doing something to make my own environment, community a better place.
q9.opt3: Helping create the foundations for a more sustainable and fair society and economy.

q10.text: How do you stand with international, transnational collaborations?
q10.opt0: I am only interested in my own micro-environment.
q10.opt1: I follow international news, but I am not part of such networks.
q10.opt2: I sometimes learn from foreign examples, or participate in an international event.
q10.opt3: I take an active role in international networks, processes, exchange knowledge and create strategies on a global level.

archetypeTest.title: Community Archetype Test
archetypeTest.description: What role do you play in changemaking?

a1.text: Where do you feel most effective in promoting change?
a1.opt0: In my own immediate environment, in local practical work.
a1.opt1: In connecting different people and groups.
a1.opt2: In achieving reforms within existing institutions or systems.
a1.opt3: In widespread communication of issues and increasing their visibility.
a1.opt4: In providing resources, support, and tools for others.
a1.opt5: In creating novel ideas and iterating innovative solutions.
a1.opt6: In shaking and transforming the foundations of obsolete, dysfunctional systems.
a1.opt7: In developing guidelines, long-term strategies, and providing advice.

a2.text: What kind of task fills you with the most energy?
a2.opt0: Manual work, local community organizing, permaculture.
a2.opt1: Facilitating negotiations and meetings between organizations.
a2.opt2: Making organizational structures, guidelines, and regulations more understandable.
a2.opt4: Writing proposals, putting together budgets, fundraising.
a2.opt6: Questioning the status quo, protests, radical innovative actions.
a2.opt7: Analyzing complex problems, system-level planning, mentoring.

a3.text: How do you view conflicts and obstacles?
a3.opt0: I try to resolve them with harmony and community building in my micro-circumstances.
a3.opt1: By nurturing relationships and building trust, I build bridges between opposing parties.
a3.opt2: Step by step, by changing existing regulations.
a3.opt3: I give voice to oppressed perspectives to initiate change through awareness raising.
a3.opt4: I can provide the necessary energy / space even if the situation is difficult.
a3.opt5: Problems are brilliant opportunities to develop new creative paths.
a3.opt6: They are a necessary accompaniment to the decay of the old, I step into confrontation if it serves the cause.
a3.opt7: I see through the motivations of the actors and suggest long-term tactics while watching the timeline.

rdgTest.title: Regenerative Development Goals (RDG)
rdgTest.description: Which areas are closest to you? (Select a maximum of 3)

rdg1.text: Check the most important maximum 3 goals for you!
rdg1.opt6: 7. Reconnection with Nature and Earth Protection
rdg1.opt8: 9. Knowledge Sharing and Open Source Education
rdg1.opt9: 10. Peaceful Cultural Transition and Community Building
rdg1.opt10: 11. Clean Water and Sanitation for All
rdg1.opt16: 17. Clean and Renewable Energy for All
rdg1.opt23: 24. Open Science and Global Knowledge Sharing
rdg1.opt24: 25. Preservation of Cultural Heritage and Human Diversity
rdg1.opt27: 28. Planetary Awareness and Earth as a Living System
